L1.area: User account lifecycle management and authentication token handling

L2.description: A cryptographically weak pseudo-random number generator (PRNG) is used for generating security-sensitive tokens in the user self-registration and password recovery mechanism. This allows an attacker to predict generated tokens within a reasonable time frame and thereby take over user accounts.

L3.area: Token generation utilities used by the user resource management layer
L3.description: A cryptographically weak pseudo-random number generator (PRNG) is used to generate recovery and activation tokens during user self-registration and password recovery. The token generation relies on a non-cryptographic random source, allowing an attacker to predict tokens within a reasonable time frame and take over user accounts.

ground_truth.reason: The recovery and activation token generation uses RandomStringUtils.randomAlphanumeric() which relies on java.util.Random, a non-cryptographic PRNG, making tokens predictable and enabling account takeover.
